title: Regression - THE PAYOFF
description: Detailed rubrics for all 6 sessions in Module 3: Regression

sessions.title: Introduction to Regression
sessions.description: Foundational regression concepts and evaluation metrics

criteria.description: Demonstrates clear understanding of regression vs classification, continuous prediction, and use cases

levels.excellent_5: Clearly articulates regression vs classification differences, explains multiple regression metrics (MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²) with examples, identifies appropriate regression use cases
levels.good_4: Explains regression concept well, understands most metrics with minor gaps, can identify regression problems
levels.adequate_3: Basic understanding of regression, some confusion on metric interpretation, limited examples
levels.poor_1-2: Significant misunderstandings of regression concept or metrics, cannot distinguish from classification

criteria.description: Successfully implements linear regression model with proper evaluation

levels.excellent_6: Code runs perfectly, achieves R² > 0.7 on test data, proper train-test split, all metrics calculated (MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²), well-commented code
levels.good_5: Code runs with minor issues, R² > 0.6, most metrics calculated, adequate comments
levels.adequate_3-4: Code runs but R² < 0.6 or missing some metrics, limited comments, some implementation issues
levels.poor_1-2: Code has significant errors, very poor performance, incomplete implementation

criteria.description: Analyzes model performance and draws meaningful conclusions

levels.excellent_4: Deep analysis comparing multiple metrics, interprets R² correctly, discusses model strengths/weaknesses, identifies improvement areas with specific suggestions
levels.good_3: Good analysis with insights about model performance, compares metrics, mentions improvements
levels.adequate_2: Basic analysis, mostly descriptive, limited interpretation
levels.poor_1: Little to no meaningful analysis, just reports numbers

sessions.title: Multiple Regression & Feature Engineering
sessions.description: Multiple features, polynomial regression, regularization, and feature scaling

criteria.description: Creates meaningful features and applies proper scaling/transformation

levels.excellent_5: Creates 3+ new features (polynomial, interactions, or domain-specific), applies appropriate scaling (StandardScaler/MinMaxScaler), justifies feature choices, removes redundant features
levels.good_4: Creates 2+ features, applies scaling correctly, reasonable feature choices
levels.adequate_3: Creates 1-2 basic features, applies scaling with minor issues
levels.poor_1-2: Minimal feature engineering, no scaling, or incorrect application

criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of overfitting and uses regularization appropriately

levels.excellent_6: Implements and compares Linear Regression, Ridge, and Lasso, tests different regularization strengths, identifies optimal model, explains overfitting risks, shows train vs test performance
levels.good_5: Implements 2+ regularization approaches, compares performance, discusses overfitting
levels.adequate_3-4: Implements one regularization method, basic comparison, limited discussion
levels.poor_1-2: No regularization or incorrect implementation, no overfitting consideration

criteria.description: Code quality, proper implementation, and results presentation

levels.excellent_4: Clean, well-organized code, proper use of sklearn pipelines or systematic workflow, clear visualizations comparing models, comprehensive results table
levels.good_3: Good code structure, successful implementation, adequate visualization
levels.adequate_2: Working code with some organization issues, basic results presentation
levels.poor_1: Poorly organized code, incomplete implementation, unclear results

sessions.title: Time Series Basics
sessions.description: Time series concepts, proper splitting, temporal features, and trend/seasonality

criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of temporal dependencies and time series concepts

levels.excellent_5: Clearly explains why order matters, identifies trends and seasonality, understands autocorrelation, describes appropriate time series models, explains why standard CV doesn't work
levels.good_4: Good grasp of temporal dependencies, identifies trends or seasonality, knows about proper splitting
levels.adequate_3: Basic understanding of time series, some confusion on key concepts
levels.poor_1-2: Treats time series like regular tabular data, doesn't understand temporal aspects

criteria.description: Implements chronological train-test split and prevents data leakage

levels.excellent_6: Perfect chronological split (train on old, test on recent), no data leakage, creates lag features correctly, validates no future information in features, documents split rationale
levels.good_5: Correct chronological split, mostly prevents leakage, creates some lag features
levels.adequate_3-4: Attempts chronological split with minor issues, some potential leakage concerns
levels.poor_1-2: Uses random split (wrong!), clear data leakage, future information in features

criteria.description: Creates time-based features and analyzes temporal patterns

levels.excellent_4: Creates multiple temporal features (lags, rolling stats, date features), visualizes trends and seasonality, decomposes time series, analyzes ACF/PACF
levels.good_3: Creates lag and rolling features, basic trend visualization, identifies patterns
levels.adequate_2: Creates basic lag features, minimal analysis
levels.poor_1: No temporal features or incorrect implementation

sessions.title: Feature Engineering for Stocks
sessions.description: Technical indicators, domain knowledge application, and financial feature engineering

levels.excellent_6: Implements 5+ indicators correctly (SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, volatility, etc.), all calculations verified, proper handling of NaN values, visualizes indicators
levels.good_5: Implements 3-4 indicators correctly, proper calculations, handles NaN
levels.adequate_3-4: Implements 2-3 indicators with minor calculation issues
levels.poor_1-2: Implements <2 indicators or major calculation errors

criteria.description: Applies financial domain knowledge to feature engineering and explains indicator significance

levels.excellent_5: Explains what each indicator measures and why it matters for prediction, discusses overbought/oversold conditions, trend identification, momentum, demonstrates understanding of trading concepts
levels.good_4: Explains most indicators, shows understanding of financial concepts
levels.adequate_3: Basic explanation of indicators, limited domain knowledge
levels.poor_1-2: No explanation or understanding of financial concepts, just mechanical implementation

criteria.description: Analyzes feature correlations and importance for stock prediction

levels.excellent_4: Creates correlation matrix, identifies multicollinearity issues, analyzes feature-target relationships, removes redundant features, explains which features should be predictive and why
levels.good_3: Analyzes correlations, identifies some important features
levels.poor_1: No feature analysis or incorrect interpretation

sessions.title: Advanced Regression Models
sessions.description: Decision trees, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and ensemble methods

criteria.description: Successfully implements and compares multiple advanced regression algorithms

levels.excellent_6: Implements all three models (Decision Tree, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting) with appropriate hyperparameters, includes baseline for comparison, tests multiple configurations, documents choices
levels.good_5: Implements 2-3 models successfully, reasonable hyperparameters, compares to baseline
levels.adequate_3-4: Implements 1-2 models, basic configuration, limited comparison

criteria.description: Systematically compares models and selects best performer with justification

levels.excellent_5: Creates comprehensive comparison table (RMSE, R², MAE, training time), visualizes predictions for all models, analyzes overfitting (train vs test), selects best model with clear justification considering accuracy AND complexity
levels.good_4: Compares models on multiple metrics, good visualization, reasonable selection
levels.adequate_3: Basic comparison on 1-2 metrics, identifies best model
levels.poor_1-2: Minimal comparison, no clear selection rationale

criteria.description: Analyzes feature importance and provides model insights

levels.excellent_4: Extracts and visualizes feature importance from tree-based models, interprets which features drive predictions, compares importance across models, validates findings against domain knowledge
levels.good_3: Extracts feature importance, basic visualization and interpretation

sessions.title: Stock Predictor PAYOFF - Building the Module 0 Demo
sessions.description: THE PAYOFF SESSION - Complete stock predictor with advanced models and deployment readiness

criteria.description: Builds end-to-end stock prediction system integrating all learned concepts

levels.excellent_6: Complete pipeline: data download, 10+ technical indicators, proper time series split, 3+ models trained, best model selected, predictions generated, model saved, all code runs flawlessly, production-quality code
levels.good_5: Complete pipeline with minor gaps, 5+ indicators, 2+ models, working predictions
levels.adequate_3-4: Mostly complete pipeline, some components missing or poorly implemented
levels.poor_1-2: Incomplete pipeline, major components missing or broken

criteria.description: Explicitly connects to Module 0 demo and demonstrates achievement of the original goal

levels.excellent_4: Clearly documents connection to Module 0, compares implementation to original demo, reflects on learning journey, celebrates achievement with specific examples of growth, articulates 'I built this!' moment
levels.good_3: Mentions Module 0 connection, shows awareness of achievement
levels.poor_1: No connection made to Module 0 or course journey

criteria.description: Achieves strong performance with proper validation

levels.excellent_3: R² > 0.75, RMSE significantly better than baseline, includes backtesting, realistic performance assessment, acknowledges limitations
levels.good_2: R² > 0.65, beats baseline, proper evaluation, realistic expectations
levels.adequate_1: R² > 0.50 or beats baseline, basic evaluation

criteria.description: Model deployment preparation and comprehensive documentation

levels.excellent_2: Model saved with versioning, clear README with setup instructions, results documented, requirements.txt included, reproducible workflow
levels.good_1: Model saved, basic documentation, mostly reproducible

special_notes: This is THE most important session - the emotional and technical culmination
Grade generously for effort and completion - celebrate the achievement!
The 'PAYOFF Recognition' criterion is unique to this session
Even if performance isn't perfect, recognize the journey and learning
This session integrates everything from Module 1 (data), Module 2 (ML), and Module 3 (regression)

passing_threshold.special_note: Session 3.6 should be graded with appreciation for the complete journey

evaluation_principles: Value proper methodology over high scores
Prevent data leakage is MORE important than perfect R² scores
Code clarity and documentation matter for real-world readiness

metadata.emphasis: Session 3.6 is THE PAYOFF - weight emotional and technical achievement equally
metadata.learning_progression: Sessions build from fundamentals (3.1) to complete application (3.6)
metadata.real_world_focus: All sessions emphasize practical application and production considerations
